levels.label: Hypothesis
levels.copy: The Product-Market Fit Evidence pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Early Signal
levels.copy: Some Product-Market Fit Evidence practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Emerging Fit
levels.copy: Core Product-Market Fit Evidence practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Strong Fit
levels.copy: Product-Market Fit Evidence practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Repeatable Fit
levels.copy: Product-Market Fit Evidence is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.retention-and-behaviour: Build cohort retention by segment and define the recurring behaviour that represents realised value.
recommendations.demand-pull-and-willingness-to-pay: Test standard pricing, renewal, and expansion, not only enthusiasm or subsidised pilots.
recommendations.segment-clarity: Choose the highest-fit segment and publish explicit non-fit criteria internally.
recommendations.repeatable-acquisition: Prove one channel repeatability with conversion, sales cycle, payback, and saturation indicators.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
